message:
fix(health): guard skip_db_open by db_path.is_file() [session-review-rojr9] (review of f3dcce6d)

Session-wide deep review of ~110 commits caught a real regression
from the gi4oy fix (commit f3dcce6d). The skip_db_open optimistic
path branched on `(skip_db_open && db_exists)`, but
`db_path.exists()` returns true for BOTH regular files AND
directories — so a corrupt-DB scenario (e.g., a directory at the
DB path) silently reported `db.opened=true` through the cass
health probe.

The corrupt-DB regression test
tests/cli_robot.rs:2725::health_json_reports_open_error_for_unopenable_db_path
creates a DIRECTORY at the DB path and asserts:
  - db.opened == false
  - db.open_error contains "Failed to open"
  - healthy == false
  - status == "degraded"

Pre-this-fix, post-gi4oy, that test would FAIL because skip_db_open
synthesized opened=true regardless of whether FrankenStorage could
actually open the path. The integrity-gate sibling of the eg613
perf gate was silently broken.

Fix: capture fs::metadata once and derive both .len() and .is_file()
from it (was already 2 syscalls via .map().ok(); now consolidated
to one). Guard the skip-open branch with `&& db_is_regular_file`.
If the DB path exists but is NOT a regular file, fall through to
probe_state_db which surfaces the failure via opened=false +
open_error. Preserves the gi4oy perf win for the normal case (regular
DB file) while restoring the integrity contract for corrupt
scenarios.

Validation: rch test pass for BOTH:
- health_json_reports_open_error_for_unopenable_db_path (this
  regression's reproducer)
- health_json_large_seeded_db_p50_stays_under_50ms (gi4oy perf gate
  preserved — still <50ms p50)

3 tests pass, 0 failed, exit=0, 73s warmed run.

Closes coding_agent_session_search-rojr9.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
